Title: MARKET PULSE PM JULY 3, 2026 [WATCH]
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Bursa Malaysia ended higher on Friday, led by a rebound in banking stocks as sentiment improved after softer-than-expected US labour market data.
Heavyweight financial shares supported the benchmark index, as concerns over further US interest rate hikes eased and boosted risk appetite.
Meanwhile, the ringgit rose 4.0670.
In the crypto market, Bitcoin rose to RM250,365.
Ethereum rose to RM7,005, while Solana was up at RM329.
That wraps up today's Market Pulse.

Title: Foreign beggar activities in KL linked to organised crime syndicates, says ministry
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Begging activities involving foreigners, especially children, are believed to be linked to organised crime syndicates, the Home Ministry said.
In a written reply published on the Parliament portal, the ministry said the Immigration Department conducted 836 enforcement operations in Kuala Lumpur and its surrounding areas between 2025 and May 31 this year.
A total of 10,609 individuals were inspected during the operations, resulting in the detention of 5,411 foreigners for various immigration offences, including 42 individuals involved in begging.
"Investigations are also being conducted against locals suspected of acting as protectors, organisers, or financial beneficiaries of these activities.
"The ministry will not compromise on any breach of the law or any form of exploitation that could affect the country's image and reputation," it said.
The ministry was replying to a question from Bukit Bintang member of parliament Fong Kui Lun regarding the effectiveness of enforcement operations against foreign beggars in tourist hotspots such as Bukit Bintang, Jalan Alor and Changkat Bukit Bintang.
To enhance enforcement, the ministry said the Immigration Department has stepped up integrated operations with the police and Kuala Lumpur City Hall (DBKL), while improving intelligence sharing to map out syndicate networks.
The Kuala Lumpur Immigration Department is also actively involved in the Federal Territory Security Working Committee and the KL Strike Force alongside the police, DBKL, the Social Welfare Department and other enforcement agencies.
Through the KL Strike Force, these agencies monitor begging and vagrancy hotspots, conduct regular and unscheduled integrated operations, and expedite intelligence sharing to prevent a recurrence.
"An integrated approach with the police, DBKL and related agencies will continue to be empowered to combat foreign beggar syndicates firmly, comprehensively and continuously to preserve public safety and the nation's image," the ministry added.
In a separate parliamentary reply, the ministry said 314 employers were recommended for foreign worker quotas out of 1,919 applications received during a special application window from Jan 19 to March 31 this year.
The special period was introduced to assist employers who were unable to submit applications before the Dec 31, 2025, deadline to obtain foreign worker quotas under the 2025 quota ceiling.
Subsequently, the employers who obtained the recommendations are awaiting deliberation by the Peninsular Malaysia Labour Department to obtain initial approval to employ foreign workers under Section 60(K) of the Employment Act 1955, it said.
The ministry was responding to Beruas member of parliament Datuk Ngeh Koo Ham, who asked about the total approved quota for employing foreign workers during that specific period.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Johor polls: Housing, environment top PSM manifesto for Skudai
Article:
JOHOR BARU: Parti Sosialis Malaysia (PSM) has unveiled its manifesto for the Skudai seat in the Johor polls, focusing on affordable housing, environmental sustainability and youth employment.
On housing, PSM pledged to push for genuinely affordable homes and rental controls aligned with the incomes of ordinary citizens, alongside more urban transit housing for young families and the working class.
The party estimated that with about 23,000 marriages taking place in Johor annually, the state requires 23,000 affordable units each year for middle-income young families.
It said the stock of low-cost urban housing was depleting due to urban renewal projects that replace existing units with high-priced apartments.
To address this, PSM proposed building more People's Housing Project (PPR) flats for rent and repurposing abandoned commercial buildings into affordable rental units.
It also recommended reforming the allocation system for PPR and transit housing to ensure transparency and community involvement.
Furthermore, the party urged that the maintenance of low-cost flats, where joint management bodies (JMB) fail to collect sufficient fees, be placed under municipal councils with adequate state funding.
Under its environmental and development pillar, PSM called for stricter standard operating procedures (SOPs) for factories and data centres to curb pollution, alongside improved maintenance of rivers around Skudai to mitigate flash floods.
The party highlighted a flaw in the current Environmental Impact Assessment (EIA) framework, noting that developers directly appoint and pay EIA consultants, which could lead to biased reports favouring project approvals.
PSM proposed that the state government take over the appointment of EIA consultants by charging developers an assessment fee and creating an independent fund to ensure objective reviews.
It also called for comprehensive strategies to tackle river siltation and surface runoff caused by converting forests or agricultural land into commercial plots.
Suggested measures include scheduled river dredging, upstream retention ponds, and using playgrounds or parking areas as temporary retention zones.
The party also advocated making public feedback on town planning transparently available on municipal websites and strengthening public transport through an electric bus network.
To address youth employment, PSM noted a structural mismatch in the national economy, where 200,000 new graduates enter the market annually but only 50,000 technical and professional jobs are created.
The party recommended promoting domestic technology manufacturing, such as building electric buses locally, and expanding positions within the "care economy" by hiring welfare workers to run healthy youth programmes at residential flats.
It also urged the expansion of green industries, including solar energy generation, household waste recycling, and river and forest restoration, to create new employment opportunities.
PSM's sole candidate in the Johor polls is 40-year-old Amir Syafiq Ameer Soekre.
The PSM secretary, who works as a sales executive, is contesting the Skudai state seat under the slogan "Skudai Saksama" (Equitable Skudai).
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Johor election: Tiram seat sees fierce four-cornered battle
Article:
JOHOR BARU: The Tiram state seat, once regarded as a traditional stronghold of Barisan Nasional, has re-emerged as a key battleground in the Johor political landscape following shifting voter sentiment in recent general and state elections.
The seat, which fell to Pakatan Harapan in the 2018 general election, was reclaimed by BN in the 2022 Johor election when its candidate Azizul Bachok secured a comfortable majority of 8,098 votes in a seven-cornered contest.
In the current state election, BN is fielding veteran politician Senator Datuk Abdul Halim Suleiman, who is also Tebrau Umno division chief, in a bid to retain the seat.
He faces a four-cornered fight against PH's Nor Zulaila Abd Ghani, Perikatan Nasional's Khirul Muntanazar Ismail, andDr Harith Fakhrudin Abdul Malek of Parti Bersama Malaysia.
The Tiram contest is expected to be closely watched, with political observers noting that while all candidates have a chance, BN appears to have an edge based on its organisational strength and the profile of its candidate.
Halim previously served two terms as the Puteri Wangsa assemblyman between 2004 and 2013 and is regarded as an experienced grassroots leader with long-standing ties in the constituency.
Known for his approachable personality and hands-on engagement with residents, supporters say his track record of constituency service remains a key factor in maintaining voter confidence across communities.
However, critics have described him as a "recycled candidate", a label he has downplayed, saying voters will ultimately judge him based on his service record.
Despite the challenge from younger contenders, BN is banking on continuity and local familiarity to retain the seat, while rival parties are expected to mount strong campaigns through their respective grassroots machinery.
The Tiram constituency has a total of 117,390 registered voters, including 106 early voters and 117,284 ordinary voters, setting the stage for a closely watched multi-cornered contest.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Ringgit ends higher against US dollar on favourable Asian currency sentiment
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The ringgit ended higher against the US dollar at the close of the trading week, supported by favourable Asian currency sentiment following the latest United States Nonfarm Payrolls (NFP) data.
At 6 pm, the local note appreciated to 4.0690/0735 against the greenback from Thursday's close of 4.0780/0830.
Bank Muamalat Malaysia Bhd chief economist Dr Mohd Afzanizam Abdul Rashid said the latest NFP data was the main catalyst, as the figure came in below expectations, suggesting that the US Federal Reserve was not in a hurry to raise its benchmark interest rate.
"The ringgit strengthened to as high as 4.0600 against the greenback during the morning session, but later pared some gains in the afternoon before settling at the current closing level," he told Bernama.
Mohd Afzanizam added that Asian currencies performed favourably against the US dollar today.
At the close, the ringgit was mostly higher against a basket of major currencies.
It rose versus the British pound to 5.4325/4385 from 5.4401/4467 at the close on Thursday, and gained against Japanese yen to 2.5253/5281 from 2.5295/5327 previously.
However, it was lower against the euro at 4.6553/6606 from 4.6526/6583 yesterday.
The local note was mostly lower against regional currencies.
It slid against the Singapore dollar to 3.1521/1558 from 3.1517/1558, and slipped against the Thai baht to 12.2782/2966 from 12.2411/2613 yesterday.
Meanwhile, it was higher against the Indonesian rupiah to 226.5/226.8 from 226.6/226.9, and unchanged against the Philippine peso at 6.62/6.63 compared to yesterday's close
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Respect Court of Appeal's full judgment on PSR project, NGO tells DAP Youth
Article:
GEORGE TOWN: Non-governmental organisation Pertubuhan Jaringan Ekologi dan Iklim (Jedi) has urged Penang DAP Youth to respect the full Court of Appeal judgment on the Penang South Reclamation (PSR) project.
Jedi, one of the appellants in the case alongside affected fishermen and Sahabat Alam Malaysia (SAM), said Penang DAP Youth should not cherry-pick the outcome of the appeal while ignoring the court's written grounds of judgment.
The group was responding to the youth wing's statement describing the term "illegal island", used by SAM to refer to the reclamation project, as
propaganda
following the dismissal of the appeal.
Jedi said it accepted the Court of Appeal's decision to dismiss the appeal, but argued that the judgment also contained significant findings on the powers of state planning authorities over reclamation involving the territorial sea and seabed.
"The issue is not the dismissal of the appeal but whether the Penang government and Penang DAP Youth are prepared to respect the court's full reasoning, including the finding that state planning authorities have no power to approve reclamation over the seabed.
"Respecting the Court decision means respecting the whole judgment and not only the part that is politically convenient," they said in a statement today.
Jedi said the Court of Appeal's findings raised legitimate questions over the legal authority relied upon to commence reclamation and grant planning permission.
They questioned whether federal authorisation, a Cabinet decision or any specific written approval had been obtained to reclaim the territorial sea and seabed.
Jedi also said approvals such as the Structure Plan, National Physical Planning Council endorsement and the Environmental Impact Assessment did not automatically answer the question of who had legal authority over the territorial sea and seabed before reclamation was completed.
The group called on the state government to disclose the legal basis and written authorisation for the reclamation if all necessary approvals had been obtained from the relevant authority.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: DBKL shuts 2 foreigner-run premises at Kuala Lumpur Wholesale Market
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Kuala Lumpur City Hall (DBKL) has ordered the immediate closure of two foreigner-operated business premises at the Kuala Lumpur Wholesale Market for operating without licences during a joint enforcement operation.
In a Facebook post today, DBKL said the overnight operation was carried out with the Immigration Department and police between 11pm on July 2 and 3am this morning, targeting wholesale businesses along Jalan 2/3A, Pusat Bandar Utara.
It said inspections covered wholesalers dealing in fruits, vegetables, frozen food and general goods, focusing on premises suspected of operating without licences, failing to display business licences, being operated by foreign nationals, obstructing public walkways, operating without signboard licences and breaching licensing conditions.
The two premises were ordered to close immediately for operating without valid business licences and being run by foreign nationals.
Enforcement action was taken under the Federal Territory of Kuala Lumpur's Trade, Business and Industrial Licensing By-Laws 2016, Street, Drainage and Building Act 1974, Advertisements By-Laws 1982 and the Local Government Act 1976.
Meanwhile, Immigration Department officers, assisted by police, also inspected foreign workers at the premises to verify their immigration status and compliance with employment regulations.
DBKL said the operation proceeded smoothly and similar integrated enforcement operations would continue from time to time to curb illegal business activities and ensure compliance with licensing regulations.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Global stocks climb as US jobs data cools Fed hike fears
Article:
LONDON/SINGAPORE: Global stocks climbed on Friday after a lukewarm US jobs report softened expectations for an imminent rate hike from the Federal Reserve and regional activity gauges pointed to an economic expansion during June.
Europe's broadest index hit a record high on Friday, and was set for its biggest weekly gain in over a month.
The pan-European STOXX 600 reached 651.77 before settling to a steadied 648.74. Germany's DAX index rose 0.1 per cent while the French and UK indices were a touch lower on the day.
MSCI's broadest index of world shares rose 0.4 per cent.
"Europe's Stoxx 600 ended the week with a bang as investors lapped up utilities, industrials and basic materials stocks," Dan Coatsworth, at investment platform AJ Bell, said in a note.
"While these movements imply a more upbeat investor, it's important to keep watching the US tech stocks, as many are coming off the boil," he added.
South Korea's Kospi swung between gains and losses before closing around 6 per cent higher, as buyers pounced on battered chipmaker stocks.
Purchasing Managers' Index (PMI) data released on Friday indicated increased activity across Asia.
Japan's services sector returned to expansion in June after stalling the previous month. China's services activity expanded at a slightly slower pace, but overseas demand rose at the fastest rate in 20 months.
"The PMIs remain healthy by recent standards and still imply stronger economic momentum across Q2 as a whole," analysts from Capital Economics said of the Chinese data.
US LABOUR MARKET COOLING
US job growth slowed sharply in June and payroll gains for the prior two months were revised lower, according to data released on Thursday, pointing to a cooling labour market.
The unemployment rate dropped to 4.2 per cent last month from 4.3 per cent in May as workers left the labour force, pushing the participation rate to the lowest level in more than five years.
The tepid jobs data doused traders' expectations of an imminent rate hike and raised the chances that the Fed will keep rates on hold until October.
Fed funds futures are pricing an implied 46.8 per cent probability that the US central bank will keep rates steady at its meeting on September 15 to 16, compared to a 35.8 per cent chance a day earlier, according to the CME Group's FedWatch tool.
Inflation remained a concern.
"Our biggest anticipated risk this year, even before the Iran war, was shipping," said James Rossiter, head of global economics at TD Securities.
"Ships have been rerouted all over the world because of the Hormuz Strait closure, leading to less shipping capacity globally," he told Reuters in a phone call, suggesting the price effects of this were still working their way through the global economy.
US futures remained buoyant, with S&P 500 and Nasdaq futures up 0.3 per cent and 1 per cent respectively. The US market will be closed on Friday to celebrate Independence Day.
Against the yen, the US dollar held steady around 161, with the greenback having given up earlier gains as market liquidity was thinned by the holiday and traders remained on watch for intervention.
The Japanese currency has been choppy this week after Reuters reported on Thursday authorities may have adopted a new approach to their forays into the market.
The US dollar index, which measures the greenback's strength against a basket of six currencies, was down 0.3 per cent at 100.71.
In commodities, Brent crude futures steadied at US$71.80. Gold was up 1.3 per cent at US$4,176.
In cryptocurrencies, bitcoin ticked up 0.1 per cent to US$61,604.53.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Rebound in banking stocks lifts Bursa Malaysia at close
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Bursa Malaysia ended Friday's trade with a bounce on the back of a rally in banking stocks as investor sentiment improved following softer-than-expected US labour market data.
IPPFA Sdn Bhd director of investment strategy and country economist Mohd Sedek Jantan said the recovery in heavyweight financial counters provided the main support for the benchmark index, reflecting increased risk appetite after concerns over further US monetary tightening eased.
At 5pm, the FTSE Bursa Malaysia KLCI (FBM KLCI) jumped 17.22 points to 1,679.05 from Thursday's close of 1,661.83.
The benchmark index opened 3.90 points higher at 1,665.73 and moved between 1,662.91 and 1,682.62 throughout the session.
The market breadth was positive, with gainers trouncing decliners 633 to 372, while 533 counters were unchanged, 1,112 untraded, and 12 suspended.
Turnover increased to 2.79 billion units valued at RM1.93 billion, up from 2.70 billion units valued at RM2.00 billion yesterday.
Mohd Sedek said market sentiment strengthened after the US June employment report showed non-farm payrolls increased by just 57,000, well below market expectations of 113,000, while payroll figures for the previous two months were revised down by a combined 74,000.
"The weaker labour market data tempered expectations that the US Federal Reserve (Fed) would need to resume interest rate hikes, reducing concerns that tighter financial conditions could weigh on global economic growth and corporate earnings.
"The cooling outlook for additional US rate hikes encouraged buying across regional equity markets, with investors rotating back into cyclical sectors, including financials, amid expectations that the Fed may maintain a more accommodative policy stance in the months ahead," he told Bernama.
Among heavyweight stocks, Maybank gained six sen to RM10.80, while CIMB added 15 sen to RM7.60,  and Tenaga Nasional climbed two sen to RM14.28. Public Bank was five sen higher at RM4.89, while IHH Healthcare was two sen lower at RM8.15.
As for the active stocks, Velesto added two sen to 28.5 sen, Zetrix AI gained one sen to 79 sen, and Silver Ridge gained four sen to 21 sen.  ACE Market debutant Eckem Holdings was flat at 12 sen, and Capital A edged down half a sen to 40.5 sen.
On the gainers' list, F&N jumped 88 sen to RM28.98, KL Kepong added 74 sen to RM21.70, and Dutch Lady garnered 34 sen to RM32.40. Batu Kawan soared 54 sen to RM21.64, and PPB bagged 21 sen to RM9.54.
Of the decliners, Nestle shed 80 sen to RM91.20, Malaysian Pacific Industries lost 28 sen to RM47.78, and United Plantations lost 36 sen to RM33.12. Far East dropped eight sen to RM3.82, and Paragon Union trimmed seven sen to RM1.70.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Johor election heats up as Anwar, Zahid and other leaders descend this weekend
Article:
JOHOR BARU: The heat of the Johor election is intensifying as top political leaders descend on the state to attend a series of campaign events and ceramahs this weekend.
The presence of Prime Minister and Pakatan Harapan chairman Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim at Wisma SBBU, Bandar Baru Uda here tomorrow night is expected to draw thousands of supporters.
This will be Anwar's first visit to Johor for the state election campaign since nomination day last Saturday.
About 7.8km away from the PH ceramah at Wisma SBBU, Barisan Nasional will also be holding a separate event featuring BN chairman and Umno president Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi.
Zahid, who is also Deputy Prime Minister, is scheduled to attend a friendly gathering with the Indian community at Perling Mall here.
[[nid:1479665]]
PH is going head-to-head against BN in all 56 seats up for grabs in the Johor election.
Southern Johor, which has a dynamic and shifting voting pattern, sees around 11 state seats in and around the city emerging as key battlegrounds between the two main coalitions.
The seats are Tiram, Puteri Wangsa, Johor Jaya, Permas, Larkin, Stulang, Perling, Kempas, Skudai, Kota Iskandar and Senai.
In the 2022 Johor election, BN won five of these seats, while the remainder were held by PH.
Meanwhile, competition in northern Johor is also heating up, with Perikatan Nasional intensifying its campaign efforts to defend the seats it previously won.
PN deputy chairman and Bersatu president Tan Sri Muhyiddin Yassin is scheduled to spend the entire day campaigning in Bukit Kepong tomorrow, after campaigning in Sedili and Panti over the past two days.
Earlier this week, PN chairman Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Samsuri Mokhtar also went on the ground, focusing his campaign in Maharani and Sungai Balang, which are key seats for the coalition.
The Election Commission has set July 11 as polling day for the 16th Johor election, while early voting will be held next Tuesday.
A total of 1,297,931 voters, or 47.6 per cent of the 2,727,926 registered voters in Johor, are aged between 18 and 39, making young voters a decisive factor in the election outcome.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Habib Jewels unveils Italian craftsmanship in gold
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: Habib Jewels Sdn Bhd is bringing the artistry of Italian jewellery-making closer to Malaysians through its Festa D'Oro 2026 campaign, which runs until July 30 across all its showrooms.
The campaign celebrates the Oro Italia 916 collection, where traditional Italian craftsmanship meets 916 gold to create jewellery that blends timeless elegance with contemporary design.
Group executive chairman Datuk Seri Meer Habib said the campaign reflects the company's commitment to offering jewellery that combines exceptional craftsmanship, lasting value and designs tailored to Malaysian lifestyles.
He said the Oro Italia 916 collection continues to bring international design excellence together with the quality and trust that Habib has built over generations.
Beyond the aesthetics, Italian jewellery expert Paolo, one of Habib's long-time manufacturing partners from Italy, said every piece begins with uncompromising quality control before the design itself takes shape.
"Quality is number one. Every single element must be checked before assembling the item. Everything has to be perfect before it comes out into the market," he said.
Paolo said crafting jewellery in 916 gold presents unique challenges because of the metal's softness compared with lower-purity gold commonly used in Europe.
"22-carat gold is not easy to work with. It is soft and delicate, so every alloy and every manufacturing step has to be carefully controlled to make it stronger and long-lasting," he said.
Among this year's highlights is the Black Cavo collection, which introduces a bold contemporary look through black rhodium plating applied over 916 yellow gold.
"Cavo is elegant, timeless and very resistant. The diamond-cut finishing gives it exceptional sparkle, and every engraving requires highly skilled craftsmanship.
"The purity always remains 916. If the black finish fades over time, it can be replated, so there is no issue with the gold itself," said Paolo.
The campaign also features refreshed designs from the Sicilia collection, inspired by the dramatic beauty of the Italian island of Sicily.
Meanwhile, the Beads Planet collection introduces handcrafted oval-shaped beads as a contemporary evolution of its signature oval-shaped bead design.
Available in yellow gold, white gold and bi-colour variations, the collection offers customers a versatile everyday style while retaining the meticulous hand-finishing synonymous with Italian craftsmanship.
For customers seeking bolder statement pieces, the Stella collection showcases wider silhouettes enhanced by precision diamond-cut finishing that captures and reflects light to maximise the brilliance of 916 gold without compromising wearability.
Making its debut this year is the Valora collection, inspired by the quiet strength and resilience of bamboo.
Paolo said the collaboration between Habib and its Italian partners spans more than two decades and has helped pioneer Italian-designed jewellery crafted in 916 gold.
He said Italy traditionally specialised in producing 18-carat jewellery before Habib challenged its craftsmen to develop the expertise required for 22-carat gold.
"More than 20 years ago, Italy mainly produced 18-carat jewellery. Habib believed there was a future for Italian-designed 22-carat jewellery and challenged us to improve our workmanship until we achieved the right result.
"Today, I believe it has become a successful choice because the designs are classic, elegant and timeless," he said.
Paolo also observed that Malaysian consumers have a different appreciation for gold compared with Europeans.
"In Europe, many young people would rather spend money on a smartphone than jewellery. Malaysians are different because they understand that gold keeps its value. It is something beautiful to wear, but it is also an investment," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Taxi commuters welcome RM10mil reform but call for better digitalisation
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Taxi commuters have welcomed the RM10 million National Madani Taxi Reform Programme but said stronger digitalisation, fares and service standards are needed to restore confidence.
Commuters interviewed by the New Straits Times said transparent fares, improved customer service and greater adoption of digitalisation remain key to persuading commuters to return to taxis.
"There is no fixed fare. It can fluctuate," said Durratun Nasihin Mazlan, 37, from Petaling Jaya, noting that fares are often negotiated verbally before a journey begins.
Wan Adam Wan Amin, 27, from Ampang, said payment methods also remain inconsistent.
"From my experience, the initial pricing is usually word-of-mouth before you accept the ride.
"It depends on the driver, but many senior drivers still don't accept QR payments," said Wan Adam.
Commuters also urged the industry to embrace app-based bookings and cashless payments to better compete with other e-hailing services.
"Just use a dedicated booking app or integrate with other e-hailing services. Everything becomes transparent," said Muhammad Amirul Shah, 31, from Shah Alam.
He said upgrading taxis alone would not change the public's perception of the services.
"You can have a brand-new car, but if the attitude stays the same, nothing will change," said Amirul.
Durratun said a rating system similar to those used by e-hailing platforms could encourage better customer service.
"Sometimes they're friendly, sometimes they're not very polite. A proper rating system would encourage better service," said Durratun.
Wan Adam also called for greater government support to help taxi drivers adapt to digital technology, saying it would allow them a fairer chance to compete with e-hailing services.
"I think the government should introduce more training or support to help taxi drivers navigate technology," said Wan Adam.
Amirul added that taxis should be more closely integrated into the public transport network, particularly as a first- and last-mile service linking commuters to rail stations.
"Taxis should be integrated into the existing public transport system as they connect areas without train stations, reducing the need for people to drive to the nearest station and improving overall accessibility," said Amirul.
Earlier today, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim approved an additional RM10 million allocation to the National Madani Taxi Reform Programme to improve taxi drivers' monthly net income by enabling lower repair, maintenance, servicing and financing costs.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Johor election: PH manifesto just expanding existing programmes, says Hishammuddin
Article:
SEMBRONG: Sembrong member of Parliament Datuk Seri Hishammuddin Hussein has dismissed Pakatan Harapan's election pledges, saying they do not amount to a manifesto.
The former Umno vice-president said the pledges outlined in the PH manifesto were merely increases in the numbers of existing programmes implemented by the Johor government under caretaker Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi.
"Many of the things presented in their manifesto are just increases in the numbers of existing state government programmes today.
"From tens of thousands of houses and so on, they are just adding to it. That is not a manifesto. A manifesto is about policies and principles," he told reporters after attending a programme with the Orang Asli community in Kahang town today.
Also present was Barisan Nasional candidate for Kahang, V. Rugendran.
Earlier today, PH launched its "Johor Untuk Semua" manifesto as its offer to voters in conjunction with the 16th Johor state election.
Among its key pledges is the Johor Health Scheme, which provides healthcare coverage of up to RM100,000 per year for the B40 and M40 groups, as well as persons with disabilities, in addition to reviving the Kad Sihat Johor programme, targeting benefits for 500,000 people.
Hishammuddin also criticised PH for, in his view, adopting BN policies and simply adding to them as part of its election pledges.
He said he was unsure whether the promises made by PH would be fulfilled.
"Wallahualam, whether they will be fulfilled or not.
"For me, that is not a manifesto. But I need to study it more carefully," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Johor election: PH manifesto offers nothing game-changing, says Zahid
Article:
JOHOR BARU: Some of the pledges announced in Pakatan Harapan's (PH) manifesto have already been implemented by the Johor government under Barisan Nasional.
BN chairman Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi said there was nothing "game-changing" in the PH manifesto.
"I have reviewed it and found that some of the pledges in the manifesto have already been implemented by the Johor government in the previous term.
"We respect what they are proposing, but Alhamdulillah, it has already been carried out by the previous Johor government.
"There is nothing new that can be considered a game changer," he told reporters after attending a community engagement programme with parents of Kemas Bangsa Johor kindergarten pupils at Larkin Court 1 flats here today.
[[nid:1479652]]
Also present was Johor BN chairman and caretaker Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi.
Earlier today, PH launched its manifesto for the 16th Johor election under the theme "Johor Untuk Semua", offering 10 key pledges including RM100,000 healthcare coverage, an RM8,000 first-home assistance scheme, a RM500 million youth fund, the construction of 80,000 homes, and 250,000 job opportunities.
Meanwhile, Zahid reminded that healthy competition is necessary in a democratic system, but comparisons between states should not be made.
This comes after some parties frequently compared Johor with Selangor.
"There is no need for such comparisons because we cannot compare apples with apples.
"Johor is far ahead not only in terms of foreign direct investment (FDI) and domestic direct investment (DDI), but also in revenue collection.
"So, while competition is good, it must be healthy," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Customs busts two syndicates, seizes RM2.57 million in illicit liquor and chewing tobacco
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Kuala Lumpur Customs Department busted two syndicates involved in processing illicit liquor and smuggling chewing tobacco, seizing uncustomed goods worth RM2.57 million in a recent special operation.
Noraidah Ishak, who is performing the duties of the Kuala Lumpur Customs director, said two foreigners were arrested during Ops Suling, which was conducted from May 11 to 23.
She said the operations team raided two warehouses along Jalan Wangsa Utama, Taman Wangsa Permai here, on May 20 and seized 4,987 litres of whisky bearing counterfeit tax stamps.
"The team also found equipment believed to have been used for processing and bottling liquor, including drums containing a chemical mixture suspected to be ethanol, rolls of counterfeit customs tax stamps, packaging equipment, bottle-capping machines and fake labels," she said in a statement.
Noraidah said the seizure was valued at RM278,531, with duties and taxes amounting to RM672,669, bringing the total value to RM951,200. The two foreign men were remanded to assist in investigations.
She said the syndicate was believed to have used warehouses located in secluded areas away from residential neighbourhoods to process and bottle the counterfeit liquor.
The case is being investigated under Section 74(1)(f) of the Excise Act 1976.
In the second case on May 14, Noraidah said the department detained a 20-foot container imported from a South Asian country at 9pm.
"Inspections uncovered 5,449kg of suspected duty-unpaid chewing tobacco products, with the seized goods estimated to be worth RM944,944, and duties and taxes amounting to RM677,551.
"The total value of the seizure in this case is estimated at RM1.62 million. The syndicate's modus operandi was to bring prohibited goods into the country in containers without a valid import licence," she said.
The case is being investigated under Section 135(1)(a) of the Customs Act 1967 for allegedly importing prohibited goods without a valid import licence.
Those with information can contact the Customs toll-free hotline at 1-800-88-8855 or at the nearest customs office.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Mitra receives 1,340 applications for Madani grant programme in 5 days
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Malaysian Indian Community Transformation Unit (Mitra) has received 1,340 applications for its Uyarvu Madani Grant Programme within five days, nearing its target of 1,500 applications.
Mitra director-general N. Raveendran Nair said the programme, launched on June 29 by Human Resources Minister Datuk Seri R. Ramanan, has drawn strong interest from Indian community entrepreneurs seeking financial assistance to expand their businesses.
He said although applications are scheduled to close on July 13, the exercise may end earlier if the target is reached before the deadline.
"Of the applications received, 700 eligible entrepreneurs who meet the programme's requirements and selection criteria will be chosen to receive the grants," he said.
The programme offers grants of up to RM50,000 to eligible Indian entrepreneurs, with assistance provided according to business categories.
The Uyarvu Madani Grant Programme is one of Mitra's initiatives aimed at enhancing the capacity and competitiveness of Indian community entrepreneurs while supporting long-term business growth.
Raveendran said the strong response reflected growing demand among micro and small entrepreneurs for financial support to strengthen their businesses.
Ramanan had previously said the programme was introduced to address financing challenges faced by micro-entrepreneurs and stressed that assistance would be distributed transparently and directly to deserving recipients.
He said Mitra was also undergoing restructuring under the Human Resources Ministry to improve delivery of community development programmes through a more systematic and integrated approach.
The transformation covers education, Technical and Vocational Education and Training, socio-economic development, healthcare and social welfare.
In a related development, the Madani government led by Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has approved an additional RM50 million allocation for Mitra, increasing the agency's annual allocation to RM150 million.
Mitra urged eligible entrepreneurs to submit applications promptly as the programme may close early once the target is reached.
Applicants must be Malaysian citizens aged between 21 and 55 with valid business registration with the Companies Commission of Malaysia.
Applications and further information are available through Mitra's official portal and social media platforms.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: DBKL cracks down on Hicks Flats over cleaning, compliance failures
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Kuala Lumpur City Hall (DBKL) has carried out an enforcement operation at Hicks, Bukit Bintang, after a follow-up inspection found continued non-compliance by the building's Joint Management Body (JMB).
The follow-up inspection was conducted today after a visit by the Kuala Lumpur mayor on June 30, 2026.
DBKL said the building management body of Hicks Flats was found to have failed to comply with directives previously issued by the authority.
As a result, a nuisance notice was issued under Section 82 of the Local Government Act 1976, ordering immediate corrective action.
DBKL also issued one compound under Section 47(1)(a) of the Street, Drainage and Building Act 1974, and another under Section 82(5) of the Local Government Act 1976 for failure to comply with cleaning directives.
To ensure cleanliness standards and protect public health, DBKL carried out cleaning works at the site, although responsibility for maintenance lies with the JMB.
"DBKL will continue to monitor compliance and take further action if the directives are ignored," it said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Indonesian man jailed 30 years for murdering wife at Sabah market
Article:
KOTA KINABALU: An Indonesian man was sentenced to 30 years' jail and 12 strokes of the rotan by the High Court today for
murdering his wife
three years ago.
Judge Datuk Alexander Siew How Wai imposed the sentence on Jedu Rudu, 45, who admitted to murdering his wife, Suria Woade, 38, also an Indonesian, at a vegetable market in Jalan Pahlawan, Kepayan, on April 8, 2023, between 1pm and 2.21pm.
The offence under Section 302 of the Penal Code carries the death penalty or a jail term of up to 40 years and, if not sentenced to death, whipping of not less than 12 strokes upon conviction.
Jedu changed his plea from not guilty to guilty when he was due to enter his defence on June 18.
In sentencing, the court ordered the accused to serve his prison term from the date of his arrest on April 8, 2023.
According to the facts of the case, the victim, the accused's estranged wife, was at the Kepayan vegetable market helping her elder sister sell vegetables.
At about 1pm, the accused arrived at the market carrying a knife before approaching the victim, who was at one of the stalls.
An argument broke out, during which the accused took out his knife and stabbed the victim in the left side of her back. She collapsed while he fled the scene.
The incident was witnessed by a vegetable trader, who called an ambulance after seeing the victim fall.
Despite efforts to save her at the scene and later at the Emergency Department of Queen Elizabeth Hospital, she was pronounced dead at 2.21pm on the same day.
A post-mortem found a single stab wound to the left side of her back, measuring approximately 17 centimetres in depth. The injury was fatal as it caused severe bleeding and breathing difficulties.
On Feb 18, the prosecution established a prima facie case against the accused, who was later ordered to enter his defence.
Nine witnesses were called during the prosecution stage of the trial.
The accused was represented by lawyers Mohamad Syazwan Shah Mohamad Ali and Nigel Neong Kaning.
Deputy public prosecutor Dacia Jane Romanus prosecuted.
The case was first brought to court on April 18, 2023.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: NRES vows stronger measures after elephant calf's death
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Natural Resources and Environmental Sustainability Ministry (NRES) is committed to strengthening mitigation measures to reduce human-wildlife conflict following the death of an elephant calf hit by a vehicle on Jalan Felda Nitar near Mersing, Johor, yesterday.
Its minister, Datuk Seri Arthur Joseph Kurup, said the ministry understood the public's reaction and emotions over the incident, adding that immediate action to manage the case had been taken by the Wildlife and National Parks Department (Perhilitan).
He said human-wildlife conflict is driven by interconnected factors, including growing human and elephant populations.
"This situation is influenced by the shrinking, fragmentation and degradation of natural habitats due to land clearing, infrastructure development, agricultural activities and land-use changes that disrupt traditional elephant migratory routes.
"In addition, crops like oil palm, bananas and sugarcane provide easily accessible food sources that lure elephants out of their natural habitats, increasing the risk of encounters with humans," he said in a statement.
Arthur said data from 2021 to May 2026 showed that 76,361 complaints regarding wildlife conflict were recorded nationwide, with estimated losses reaching RM58.7 million.
"In response, the ministry has implemented various initiatives to reduce human-elephant conflict, including the Ecological Fiscal Transfer for Biodiversity Conservation (EFT).
"Other measures include installing the Elephant Electric Fencing System (SPEG) in high-risk areas to protect residents."
NRES is also developing the Johor Elephant Sanctuary (JES) in phases as a strategic initiative to manage elephant habitats systematically.
"To curb elephant movements into residential and agricultural areas, the ministry plans to build an additional 21 food banks in Johor to complement the three existing locations," he said.
He added that the government provides the Property and Crop Loss Assistance scheme to help owners who suffer losses from wildlife attacks, including elephants.
Arthur said the ministry is also reinforcing other measures such as elephant translocation operations, the development of wildlife corridors, research and the implementation of the National Elephant Conservation Action Plan (NECAP) 2.0 to ensure human-wildlife conflicts are handled effectively and sustainably.
He urged all parties to view the incident holistically and work together to balance development, human safety and wildlife conservation.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Malaysia, Thailand boost border cooperation following Tak Bai blast
Article:
RANTAU PANJANG: Malaysia and Thailand have agreed to strengthen operational cooperation along the Sungai Golok border to curb cross-border crime and prevent security spillovers following the recent bomb blast in Tak Bai that injured two Malaysians.
General Operations Force (GOF) Southeast Brigade commander Senior Assistant Commissioner Ahmad Radzi Hussain said both countries would enhance direct communication between security commanders on the ground to enable faster responses to border issues.
He said the move followed a courtesy visit by a delegation from the Royal Thai Army's Neighbouring Countries Coordination Centre (NCCC), led by its deputy chief, General Surathep Nukaeow.
The Thai delegation is introducing the newly established NCCC, which was formed about a year ago, to security agencies in neighbouring countries, including Malaysia.
"Before meeting us, they also paid courtesy calls on the Kelantan menteri besar and the Kelantan police chief.
"Today's discussion focused on how Malaysian and Thai security forces, particularly the GOF units stationed along Golok River, can work more closely together," he told reporters after receiving the delegation here today.
Ahmad Radzi said the discussions focused on strengthening efforts to combat cross-border crimes such as drug trafficking, illegal immigration and firearms smuggling, rather than any specific incident.
"Our objective is to ensure that any unwanted elements arising from incidents such as the one in Tak Bai cannot easily infiltrate Malaysia.
"We will continue using existing bilateral mechanisms for policy matters, but operational issues can now be resolved much faster through direct communication between commanders on both sides of the border," he said.
Ahmad Radzi added that Thai authorities had expressed concern over the Tak Bai attack and assured Malaysia that the safety of Malaysian visitors remained a priority.
"They have given their assurance that Malaysians will continue to be protected. They have also informed us that appropriate assistance, including compensation for the two Malaysian victims injured in the blast, will be provided.
"The Thai authorities are also tightening security along their side of the border, just as we are doing on our side, to curb cross-border crime and other illegal activities," he said.
He said closer cooperation between the security agencies would improve border management, regional security and public confidence.
The GOF is responsible for safeguarding Malaysia's land borders, including the Kelantan-Thailand frontier along Sungai Golok, by conducting 24-hour patrols, operating checkpoints and monitoring illegal crossing points.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: MOH overhauls emergency triage system with new 5-tier scale
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Health Ministry (MOH) is revamping the triage system at public hospital emergency departments with the Malaysian Triage Scale (MTS) 2022 to assess patients more accurately and cut treatment delays.
Under the new MTS 2022, MOH has upgraded the previous three-tier colour-coded system under the Malaysian Triage Category (2011) to a more refined five-tier scale, ranging from Level 1 (Resuscitation) to Level 5 (Routine).
The ministry said the new system enhances assessment precision by dividing the process into Primary Triage (quick first-glance evaluation) and Secondary Triage (detailed assessment with vital signs).
It also introduces separate parameters specifically for paediatric patients, recognising their distinct physiological needs.
"This overhaul ensures a much more specific segregation of cases, reduces unnecessary overcrowding, and ensures that chronic patients do not fall through the cracks," MOH said in a written reply published on the Parliament portal.
The ministry was responding to a question from Datuk Seri Hishammuddin Tun Hussein (BN-Sembrong), who had asked about immediate intervention measures to revamp the emergency triage rating system at public hospital emergency departments, following recent viral incidents concerning delayed treatment of chronic cases, to prevent the loss of life.
The triage system is a clinical assessment process designed to categorise patients based on the severity of their condition and the urgency of their need for treatment. It is not on a "first come, first served" basis.
To strengthen governance and staff competency, MOH has established state-level Emergency Triage Service Technical Committees tasked with conducting cross-hospital clinical audits, evaluating current practices and running training programmes at least twice a year.
"Digital tools like the MyTriage App are also being used as a decision-making aid and training support," the ministry said.
MOH is also closely monitoring the undertriage rate as a key performance indicator to ensure rating quality remains optimal and to minimise the risk of human error.
To reduce overcrowding in emergency departments, MOH has rolled out new patient flow management guidelines effective June.
These include tightening the Non-Critical (Green) Zone policy by redirecting non-emergency cases to health clinics and private facilities through public-private initiatives such as the Madani Medical Scheme and the Healthcare Scheme for the B40 Group (PeKa B40).
In addition, emergency physicians are now given full authority to admit patients to wards within a maximum of four hours if the primary treatment team is delayed to ensure critical patients are not left waiting unnecessarily.
"MOH views this issue as a reflection of the entire service chain. The triage overhaul, backed by strict monitoring and efficient patient flow management, reflects our commitment to ensuring such incidents do not happen," the ministry said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Man injured after durian tree crashes onto house
Article:
YAN: A man was injured after a durian tree crashed onto his house in Kampung Permatang Keramat here today.
Ku Osman Ku Hamid, who is in his 40s, was trapped inside the upper section of the house after the tree struck the structure.
The Kedah Fire and Rescue Department said it received an emergency call at 10.07am and dispatched six firefighters from the Yan station, led by Senior Fire Officer II Bahari Ahmad.
The team used specialised rescue equipment to extricate the victim, who sustained injuries to his right leg and left hand.
He was handed over to Health Ministry personnel for treatment before the operation concluded at 10.46am.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: WWF urges faster wildlife crossings after elephant calf death
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The World Wide Fund for Nature (WWF) Malaysia has urged the expedited implementation of wildlife crossings in high-risk areas following the death of an elephant calf after it was hit by a vehicle on Jalan Felda Nitar, Mersing, on Tuesday.
WWF Malaysia said in a statement that roads have become deadly traps each year for both wildlife and road users.
It said solutions including wildlife corridors, eco-friendly tunnels and overpasses should be implemented more quickly.
"These structures allow animals to cross roads safely and are important for maintaining elephant migration routes. This step is also important for enriching habitats and improving forest connectivity," it said.
WWF Malaysia said the incident also resulted in serious injuries to a driver and property damage.
"The incident once again saw an elephant mother staying beside her seriously injured calf before action was taken by the authorities. This incident recalls last year's Mother's Day tragedy, when an elephant calf was killed after being hit by a lorry on the Gerik-Jeli Highway.
"This is not an isolated incident, but part of a very worrying pattern," it said.
WWF said it remains actively involved in promoting long-term solutions to the issue.
It said that following last year's tragedy, WWF Malaysia, together with the Natural Resources and Environmental Sustainability Ministry and the Department of Wildlife and National Parks, initiated a roundtable on safer roads for humans and wildlife in July 2025.
According to WWF, the initiative aims to identify more comprehensive solutions to reduce wildlife-related road accidents.
"Wildlife protection is a shared responsibility, while road users also play an important role.
"Drivers need to be more careful when passing through wildlife crossing areas by complying with speed limits, being alert and paying attention to animal crossing signs," it said.
It added that such vigilance is especially important in the early morning and at dusk when animal movement is highest.
"Driving recklessly or exceeding speed limits in high-risk areas puts the lives of humans and wildlife in unnecessary danger.
"Speed limits are only effective if consistently enforced," it said.
It also called for strict penalties for speeding offences, and expanded installation of wildlife crossing signs, speed reduction measures and smart warning systems.
The media previously reported that an elephant calf died after being hit by a vehicle, while its mother refused to move for seven hours on Jalan Felda Nitar, Mersing, early Tuesday morning.
In viral social media videos, the female elephant was seen refusing to leave her calf after it was hit at 2.28am.
Johor Perhilitan confirmed that the five-year-old elephant calf died as a result of the accident.
The driver was reported trapped and injured in the leg after the vehicle skidded and fell into a 5m ravine.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Ringgit faces near-term headwinds, medium-term outlook still positive: Standard Chartered
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The ringgit is expected to remain under near-term pressure due to external uncertainties and volatile portfolio flows but its medium-term prospects remain positive, according to Standard Chartered Global Research.
The assessment follows Bank Negara Malaysia's Financial Markets Committee's (FMC) latest statement on developments in the foreign exchange market after the ringgit came under renewed pressure against the US dollar.
Standard Chartered said the FMC's statement was likely prompted by the ringgit's recent underperformance and the pace of its depreciation.
"However, compared to previous statements by the FMC during periods of ringgit depreciation, the statement suggests that Bank Negara is not too concerned," it said.
Unlike previous statements issued during periods of ringgit depreciation, Standard Chartered said the FMC did not state that the currency's movement was inconsistent with Malaysia's economic fundamentals, excessive in nature or indicative of an undervalued exchange rate.
"The less concerned tone from the FMC is understandable as at the current level, ringgit nominal effective exchange rate is still seven per cent above its last 10 year average; similar for real effective exchange rate as well.
"So from a valuations perspective, ringgit does not screen as undervalued," it added.
Standard Chartered said the decline in Malaysia's import cover to a three-month moving average of 3.2 months as at April was mainly due to stronger import growth rather than a reduction in the country's international reserves.
However, it said the smaller buffer could make the central bank more cautious in using its reserves.
"Looking ahead, ringgit may remain vulnerable in the near term amid external headwinds and portfolio-flow volatility.
"Bank Negara is likely to step up market engagement and smooth excessive volatility, but we do not expect it to defend a specific level forcefully as long as market conditions remain orderly.
"Over the medium term, we remain constructive on the ringgit, supported by Malaysia's resilient external position and still-favourable macro fundamentals," it added.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Taxi group: New scheme cheaper than leasing, boosts ownership
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Gabungan Teksi SeMalaysia has welcomed the National Madani Taxi Programme and the additional RM10 million allocation for the matching grant scheme to replace ageing vehicles.
Its president, Kamarudin Mohd Hussain, said the initiative offers a lower daily financing structure compared with the existing leasing system.
He also welcomed the special approval from the Finance Ministry, which ensures that vehicle financing carries full ownership status for drivers.
"Today is a historic day in the taxi industry, as today we can see and witness Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim launching the taxi rejuvenation programme," he told reporters after the launch here today.
Kamarudin said about 800 taxi drivers have applied to join the rejuvenation programme, though most are still in the process of obtaining permits from the Land Public Transport Agency.
He said applicants must first be registered with the Companies Commission of Malaysia and hold a public service vehicle licence.
"Once the permits are approved, we will work with Proton for financing arrangements, which will liaise with three banks, Maybank, AmBank and Bank Muamalat, as I understand it.
"For the financing structure, the drivers will negotiate directly with banks on repayment terms; it could be RM38 or RM48 a day. It is simpler and cheaper than existing taxi leasing arrangements, he said.
Kamarudin added that elderly drivers who face difficulties securing financing could involve their children in the application process.
"In such cases, the child can be registered as the primary driver, while the parent becomes the secondary driver and they can take turns driving.
"This is better because we understand that when people are older, it is difficult to get bank financing," he said.
He said the new Proton S70 taxis are expected to follow Penang taxi fare rates, with a flagfall of RM4 and a charge of 15 sen per kilometre, as the Transport Ministry is currently studying a fare revision.
"Drivers have been asking for higher fares, and this initiative aims to help improve their income.
"The government cannot simply announce fare increases as it must go through a proper study, which is ongoing. So for now, Penang rates are being used, he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Penang economy grows 7.3pct in 2025, driven by manufacturing, services
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: Penang's economy expanded 7.3 per cent in 2025, with gross domestic product (GDP) rising to RM130.3 billion from RM121.4 billion in 2024.
The Department of Statistics Malaysia (DOSM) said the state's economic growth was driven mainly by the manufacturing and services sectors, which together accounted for about 94 per cent of Penang's GDP.
Penang's economy continues to be anchored by these two key sectors, with manufacturing accounting for more than 46 per cent of the economy, while services contribute about 48 per cent.
The manufacturing sector remains the state's largest economic driver, reinforcing Penang's position as one of Malaysia's key industrial hubs.
Its strong industrial base has earned the state the reputation as the "Silicon Valley of the East".
Renowned globally for its advanced technology ecosystem, Penang is a leading centre for the electrical and electronics industry, supported by a well-established network of multinational companies, skilled talent and robust supply chains.
"Penang's economy recorded a growth of 7.3 per cent in 2025, with the GDP value rising to RM130.3 billion from RM121.4 billion in the previous year," chief statistician Datuk Seri Dr Mohd Uzir Mahidin said.
The manufacturing sector remained the state's largest economic contributor, accounting for 47.3 per cent of GDP with a value added of RM61.7 billion.
The sector expanded by 10 per cent in 2025, compared with four per cent in 2024, led by a 12.7 per cent increase in the production of electrical, electronic and optical products.
The production of non-metallic mineral products, basic metals and fabricated metal products also supported growth, rising 8.7 per cent.
The services sector recorded value added of RM60.8 billion, up from RM58.4 billion in 2024, reflecting growth of 4.1 per cent.
The expansion was supported by a 5.6 per cent increase in the wholesale and retail trade, food and beverage, and accommodation subsectors, as well as 2.7 per cent growth in the utilities, transportation and storage, and information and communication technology subsectors.
The construction sector grew 13.4 per cent to RM4.3 billion, contributing 3.3 per cent to the state's GDP, driven by strong performances in the civil engineering and special trade subsectors.
Meanwhile, the agriculture sector grew 1.8 per cent, supported by a 3.4 per cent increase in the fisheries subsector, while mining and quarrying expanded 1.1 per cent.
Penang's GDP per capita rose to RM80,584 in 2025 from RM76,107 a year earlier, remaining well above the national average of RM59,167.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Suspended soldier, 3 others plead not guilty to perfume investment scam
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: A suspended soldier pleaded not guilty in the magistrate's court today to cheating a colleague out of RM38,400 through a non-existent perfume investment scheme.
Puteri Alisa Nabila Muhammad Razali, 23, was jointly charged with Mohammad Nazirul Azri Fairus, 29, Mariam Najwa Mahadi, 27, and Khor Leong Weong, 48.
All four claimed trial before Magistrate Faezahnoor Hassan.
They were accused of deceiving Muhammad Adam Haikal Abdul Rahman, 20, into joining the fictitious scheme and inducing him to transfer RM38,400 online into Mariam Najwa's bank account.
The alleged offence was committed at a restaurant in Sentul on May 2.
The charge, framed under Section 420 of the Penal Code read together with Section 34, carries a jail term of between one and 10 years, whipping and a fine.
Deputy public prosecutor Mohamad Fadhly Mohd Zamry asked the court to set bail at RM50,000 for Puteri Alisa Nabila and RM10,000 each for the three other accused, with one surety each.
He said the proposed bail amounts were reasonable given the sum involved in the alleged offence.
"The prosecution is seeking a higher bail for Puteri Alisa Nabila because she is a soldier and poses a flight risk, having been absent without leave for one year, five months and four days.
"We also ask that all accused surrender their passports to the court pending disposal of the case," he said.
In mitigation, lawyer Muhammad Naqib Aiman Mohd Faizal, who represented all four accused, sought lower bail on the grounds that his clients had no previous convictions.
"They are the sole breadwinners of their families, have permanent addresses and are not flight risks.
"They also cooperated throughout the investigation," he said.
After hearing submissions from both parties, Faezahnoor granted bail of RM14,000 with one surety for Puteri Alisa Nabila, and RM7,000 with one surety each for Nazirul, Mariam Najwa and Khor.
The court also ordered all four accused to surrender their passports and fixed Aug 21 for mention.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Kelantan residents keep crossing into Thailand despite bomb blast
Article:
RANTAU PANJANG: Residents in Kelantan's border towns continued crossing into southern Thailand as usual today despite a roadside bomb blast that injured two Malaysians earlier this week.
A check by the New Straits Times at the Rantau Panjang Immigration, Customs, Quarantine and Security (ICQS) Complex found traffic flowing normally, with a steady stream of pedestrians and vehicles crossing the Malaysia-Thailand border throughout the morning.
Border traffic remained steady four days after a bomb exploded near Tak Bai, Narathiwat, in an attack believed to have targeted a Thai security volunteer vehicle.
Trader Wan Mohd Faizal Wan Ismail, 43, who crosses into Sungai Golok several times a week to purchase goods, said the incident had not changed his routine.
"I was shocked when I heard about the explosion, but such incidents are isolated.
"We monitor the security situation and avoid areas considered risky. For us, business must continue," he said.
Housewife Noraini Hassan, 51, said she travelled to Sungai Golok to visit relatives and buy household items.
"My family was worried at first, but we have been crossing the border for years. As long as the authorities say the situation is under control, we will continue with our usual trips," said the mother of six.
Restaurant operator Muhammad Hafiz Rosli, 35, said many Kelantanese have close family ties and businesses across the border.
"People here are used to travelling between the two countries. We are more careful now, but we are not living in fear. Life has to go on," he said.
Thai and Malaysian authorities have since stepped up security and border patrols following the blast, while saying legal border crossings remain safe for travellers.
On Monday, two Malaysian tourists — Abdullah Syarapi Abd Rahman, 45, and Muhammad Yusri Udin, 38 — were injured when a roadside bomb exploded in Thailand's southern Narathiwat province.
The pair were treated at a hospital in Narathiwat before being transferred to Raja Perempuan Zainab II Hospital in Kota Baru.
The blast occurred against the backdrop of a long-running insurgency in southern Thailand, where thousands of people have been killed in violence that has plagued the border region for more than 20 years
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Court ruling clear, reclamation project to proceed, says Chow
Article:
BALIK PULAU: The public should not be misled or confused over the Court of Appeal's ruling on the Penang South Reclamation (PSR) project.
Chief Minister Chow Kon Yeow said the appellate court had dismissed the appeal and that was the only fact that should be accepted unless the matter was taken to a higher court.
"I believe no party should question the court's decision. If any party is dissatisfied, they can use the legal process to bring the matter before a higher court.
"All the statements being made are merely individual interpretations. The fact remains that the Court of Appeal dismissed the appeal. That is the reality.
"Whatever is stated in the judgment, any party that is dissatisfied can bring the matter to the next level of court," he told reporters at SJK (C) Sin Min in Sungai Pinang here today.
Yesterday, Sahabat Alam Malaysia (SAM) accused the Penang government of
misleading the public
over the Court of Appeal's ruling on the PSR project.
SAM honorary secretary S. Mageswari said the state government's response to the court decision misrepresented what was actually decided by the appellate court.
Her remarks followed a statement by state Infrastructure, Transport and Digital Committee chairman Zairil Khir Johari, who denied that it lacked authority to approve the project and described such claims as inaccurate and misleading.
Asked whether the project could now proceed legally, Chow said differing interpretations should instead be tested in court.
"That is an interpretation. If you want the answer to that question, go to the next court," he said.
Chow, however, maintained that, from the state's standpoint, the project would proceed.
"As far as the state is concerned, the project will proceed because there is no injunction or any order stopping it.
"At the High Court stage, the decision did not prevent us from continuing with the project because there was no injunction whatsoever," he said.
On June 30, the Court of Appeal dismissed an appeal by seven fishermen, together with SAM and Jaringan Ekologi dan Iklim, against the planning permission granted for the PSR project.
The group pointed to Paragraph 49 of the judgment, saying the appellate court upheld the High Court's decision on the basis that the first and/or second respondents lacked the authority to grant planning permission, and/or that the judicial review application had been filed outside the three-month statutory deadline.
According to SAM, the Court of Appeal's reasoning differed from that of the High Court, which had ruled that the planning permission was validly issued under the Town and Country Planning Act 1976 before dismissing the judicial review as time-barred.
SAM said the appellate court had found that the seabed and territorial waters remained under federal jurisdiction until the reclamation was completed.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Bursa achieves  FTSE4Good ESG top score
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Bursa Malaysia Bhd has become the first Malaysian public listed company to achieve the highest possible score of 5.0 under the FTSE4Good ESG assessment framework and index methodology.
The stock exchange company said this was driven by its strong performance in the climate change category.
"This is attributed to Bursa Malaysia's enhanced sustainability disclosures across environmental, social and governance (ESG) matters, particularly in climate-related reporting aligned with internationally recognised standards such as IFRS S2," it said in a statement.
Its chief executive officer Datuk Fad'l Malaysia said the achievement reflects Bursa Malaysia's progress in strengthening its ESG practices and disclosures.
"As the national Exchange, we believe the standards we encourage across the market must first be reflected in our own practices.
"It is a standard that Bursa Malaysia is committed to maintaining, and one that reinforces the importance of ESG reporting in helping investors understand the risks, opportunities and drivers of long-term value creation," he said.
The achievement places Bursa Malaysia among a select group of companies in the FTSE4Good universe, which assesses more than 12,000 companies globally.
The FTSE4Good benchmark, launched in 2001 by FTSE Russell, is one of the world's most recognised ESG benchmarks.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Home Ministry: Foreign begging cases may involve syndicates, 42 detained in KL operations
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, July 3 — Begging activities involving foreigners, especially children, may involve organised crime and are believed to be linked to syndicates, said the Ministry of Home Affairs (KDN).
It said from 2025 until May 31 this year, the Immigration Department carried out 836 enforcement operations in Kuala Lumpur and surrounding areas, inspecting 10,609 individuals. Among them, 5,411 foreigners were detained for immigration offences, including 42 involved in begging.
“Investigations are also being conducted against locals suspected of acting as protectors, organisers, or financial beneficiaries of these activities.
“The ministry will not compromise on any breach of the law or any form of exploitation that could affect the country’s image and reputation,” KDN said in a written reply published on the Parliament portal.
The ministry was replying to a question from Fong Kui Lun (PH-Bukit Bintang), who wanted to know the effectiveness of enforcement operations against begging activities by foreigners, including those involving children in tourist hotspots such as Bukit Bintang, Jalan Alor, and Changkat Bukit Bintang, as well as measures to identify and curb syndicates exploiting the group.
The ministry said to enhance enforcement, the Immigration Department has increased integrated operations with the Royal Malaysia Police (PDRM) and Kuala Lumpur City Hall (DBKL), and improved intelligence sharing and analysis to identify syndicate networks, modus operandi, and begging hotspots.
The KDN added that the Kuala Lumpur Immigration Department is also actively involved in the Federal Territory Security Working Committee Meeting and the KL Strike Force team alongside PDRM, DBKL, the Department of Social Welfare (JKM), and other enforcement agencies.
Through the KL Strike Force, the agencies identify and monitor begging and vagrancy hotspots, conduct regular and unscheduled integrated operations, expedite sharing of intelligence and public complaints, and continuously monitor to prevent recurrence, it said.
“KDN emphasises that an integrated approach with PDRM, DBKL, and related agencies will continue to be empowered to combat foreign beggar syndicates firmly, comprehensively, and continuously to preserve public safety and the nation’s image,” the KDN added.
Meanwhile, KDN said 314 employers were considered and recommended for foreign worker quotas out of 1,919 applications received during the special application period from Jan 19 to March 31 this year.
The ministry said the special period was introduced to assist employers unable to submit applications before the December 31, 2025 deadline to obtain foreign worker quotas under the 2025 quota ceiling.
“Subsequently, the employers who obtained the recommendations are currently awaiting deliberation by the Department of Labour Peninsular Malaysia (JTKSM) to obtain initial approval to employ foreign workers under Section 60(K) of the Employment Act 1955 (Act 265),” the ministry said.
The ministry was responding to a question from Datuk Ngeh Koo Ham (PH-Beruas), who asked about the total approved quota for employing foreign workers from Jan 19 to March 31 this year. — Bernama

Title: Anwar: JS‑SEZ on track with federal-state cooperation, urges leaders not to politicise issue
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, July 3 — The Johor-Singapore Special Economic Zone (JS-SEZ) is progressing according to plan and involves close cooperation between the federal and state governments, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said.
“This is a federal government project, naturally in collaboration with the state government. The matter of announcements and signing is between the Prime Minister of Malaysia and the Prime Minister of Singapore,” he told reporters after Friday prayers at Masjid As-Sodiqin, Taman Kobena, here today.
Anwar was responding to Johor Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi, who yesterday urged the federal government to expedite the announcement of the JS-SEZ master plan to sustain Johor’s economic growth momentum.
The Prime Minister also reminded political leaders not to politicise matters related to the JS-SEZ, adding that issues raised have already been clarified by the Ministry of Economy. — Bernama

Title: Zahid urges all parties to avoid old, unrelated issues in Johor campaign
Article:
JOHOR BAHRU, July 3 — Barisan Nasional (BN) chairman Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi has reminded all parties to stop playing up old issues unrelated to Johor during the state election campaign period, to ensure a mature and respectful political contest.
The Deputy Prime Minister said campaign focus should instead be on candidates’ strengths and their offers to the people, noting that some of the contesting parties are also cooperating at the federal government level.
“Although there are certain individuals who have been attacking Umno and BN with old issues, I hope these issues will not be played up because we are partners at the federal level, and I hope we approach the political arena in Johor with wisdom and in the best possible manner.
“I don’t think we need to raise other issues because every week we meet at the Cabinet meeting, and I do not want them to feel awkward when meeting leaders who are their colleagues in the Cabinet,” he said after attending a community programme in the Kempas state constituency today.
Ahmad Zahid also denied claims that BN is overconfident in facing the state election, saying the party considers itself the underdog, based on changes in the state’s political landscape.
He said BN must work hard to improve on its performance of capturing 40 seats in the last state election, especially as more than half of the voters are now young people.
According to him, the manifesto presented by Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi places emphasis on youth needs, including job opportunities and skills development through Technical and Vocational Education and Training.
Noting that the unemployment rate has fallen to 2.9 per cent, Ahmad Zahid, who is also Minister of Rural and Regional Development, said this is a positive sign, but young people still need access to premium-wage jobs that can be achieved through skills training.
“What we are offering in Johor is technical and skills training for young people, with high opportunities being given strong emphasis in the Barisan Nasional manifesto,” he said.
He therefore appealed to young voters in Johor to support BN candidates in the state election, taking into account the offerings provided for their future.
BN is contesting all 56 seats in the 16th Johor state election.
Polling will be held on July 11, with early voting on July 7.
For the latest updates on the 16th Johor state election, visit
https://prn.bernama.com/johor/
. — Bernama

Title: Perdana Park returns: Tanjung Aru’s favourite sunset spot reopens this Saturday
Article:
KOTA KINABALU, July 3 — Perdana Park in Tanjung Aru is making its comeback tomorrow, after a prolonged and sporadic closure that began nearly two years ago.
The announcement follows a pre-opening inspection led by Mayor Datuk Seri Dr Sabin Samitah, who walked the grounds yesterday to ensure the park is ready for the public.
The reopening marks a significant transition in governance, as management of the park has been officially handed over from the Sabah Ministry of Finance to the Kota Kinabalu City Hall (DBKK).
Visitors can access the park daily from 7am to 7pm. In a welcome move for regulars, parking will remain free until a digital payment system is implemented.
However, the return is a phased one. DBKK warned that several areas remain off-limits while safety assessments continue. These include the musical and interactive fountains, the water dispenser system, and the children's playground adjacent to the operations office.
DBKK also said that upgrading works will continue in stages to enhance visitor comfort and safety.
The public is also urged to exercise caution on the synthetic jogging track and in the playground, particularly during wet weather when surfaces can become slippery. To maintain order, activities such as fishing, swimming, barbecuing, camping, and hawking remain strictly prohibited, while any organised events now require prior approval from DBKK.
The park's journey to reopening has been fraught with setbacks. The original closure was triggered on September 11, 2024, after a series of otter attacks left two joggers injured, one severely.
While a reopening was initially slated for November of that year, inspections revealed a deeper problem: the park's infrastructure had fallen into extensive decay.
By December 2024, the park was closed indefinitely. A comprehensive audit uncovered a litany of failures, from rusted playground equipment and worn-out jogging tracks to broken fencing and non-functional water features. These issues drew sharp criticism from the local community, for whom the park is a cherished space for families and sunset seekers.
Originally opened in 2014 as a corporate social responsibility gift from Timatch Sdn Bhd to the Sabah state government, Perdana Park was a cornerstone of the Tanjung Aru Eco Development (TAED) initiative. For years, Timatch maintained the facility free of charge, covering substantial overheads, including RM200,000 a month for the fountains alone.

Title: Penang airport expansion records strong progress, project slated for 2028 completion, says exco
Article:
GEORGE TOWN, July 3 — The expansion of the Penang International Airport is progressing according to schedule, with several key work packages recording encouraging progress and the overall project expected to be completed in 2028.
